summary: Decompiled two Super Scope item functions in itsscope.c, updated related prototypes, added an itsscopebeam include, and refined itSScopeAttributes padding into a float array used for charge-cost indexing. The PR body and bot report claim both functions reached 100% objdiff match and main.dol SHA1 verification passed.

key_files.role: Replaced placeholders for it_80291F14 and it_80291FA8 with C implementations; added itsscopebeam.h include; used GET_ITEM, GET_ATTRS, charge-cost logic, beam spawn call, and PAD_STACK.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itsscope.c hunk adds include "itsscopebeam.h" and implementations for it_80291F14 and it_80291FA8
human_pr_text: summary says decompile it_80291F14 and it_80291FA8 in itsscope.c

key_files.role: Updated recovered function prototypes, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for it_80291D38, it_80291F14, and it_80291FA8.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itsscope.h changes it_80291D38 to int(Item_GObj*, int), it_80291F14 to void(Item_GObj*, int), and it_80291FA8 to void(Item_GObj*, Vec3*, int, float)

key_files.role: Split itSScopeAttributes padding at offset 0xC into float xC[10], enabling typed indexed access in it_80291F14.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itCommonItems.h changes pad_xC[0x28] to float xC[10]
human_pr_text: body says padding was split in itSScopeAttributes to expose xC float array

what_changed.text: Implemented it_80291F14 as a void function taking Item_GObj* and int charge_level; it reads Super Scope attributes, selects a cost from attrs->xC by charge level, subtracts it from ip->xD4C, clamps xD4C to zero, and uses PAD_STACK(16).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itsscope.c implementation of it_80291F14

what_changed.text: Implemented it_80291FA8 as a void function taking Item_GObj*, Vec3*, int charge_level, and float scale; it gets a cost from it_80291D38, subtracts and clamps ip->xD4C, then calls it_80298DEC(ip->owner, pos, charge_level, scale), with PAD_STACK(8).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itsscope.c implementation of it_80291FA8

what_changed.text: Changed itSScopeAttributes at offset 0xC from char padding of 0x28 bytes to float xC[10].
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itCommonItems.h struct change

what_changed.text: Replaced unknown prototypes in itsscope.h with concrete signatures for it_80291D38, it_80291F14, and it_80291FA8.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itsscope.h prototype changes

what_changed.text: The loaded bot report records two new matches in main/melee/it/items/itsscope: it_80291F14 at +148 bytes and it_80291FA8 at +136 bytes, each moving from 0.00% to 100.00%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] issue_comment report for GALE01

smart_moves.text: Recovered a typed float array in itSScopeAttributes instead of continuing to treat offset 0xC as opaque padding, allowing charge-level cost indexing through attrs->xC.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: padding split to expose xC float array
raw_diff: itCommonItems.h pad_xC[0x28] to float xC[10]
raw_diff: itsscope.c uses attrs->xC[charge_level]

smart_moves.text: Updated the public item header prototypes alongside the recovered function bodies, reducing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS use for the touched Super Scope functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itsscope.h prototype changes

smart_moves.text: Added the itsscopebeam.h include where the decompiled body calls it_80298DEC, keeping the dependency visible in the C source.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itsscope.c include addition and call to it_80298DEC

decomp_lessons.lesson: When decompiling item behavior that indexes into previously opaque item attributes, a small typed-struct recovery can be necessary for readable and matching source.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: padding split to expose xC float array
raw_diff: itCommonItems.h exposes float xC[10]
raw_diff: itsscope.c indexes attrs->xC in it_80291F14

decomp_lessons.lesson: Recovered function bodies should be paired with truthful header prototypes when the signature becomes known.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itsscope.h replaces UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for recovered functions
decomp_standards: truthful-headers-and-includes

naming_conventions.name: xC

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK in narrow function scope: PAD_STACK(16) in it_80291F14 and PAD_STACK(8) in it_80291FA8.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK calls at ends of new implementations
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: PR body and bot report provide match evidence, but the slice does not include detailed objdiff output explaining why PAD_STACK was needed.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This is a concrete control-flow shape in the diff; no reviewer discussion explains whether alternate forms were tried.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used a typed attribute array to avoid raw pointer arithmetic for offsets under itSScopeAttributes.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover typed item-attribute arrays when indexed access appears in matched functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2291 shows a Super Scope decomp where itSScopeAttributes padding at offset 0xC was split into float xC[10], enabling it_80291F14 to index attrs->xC by charge_level instead of using opaque padding or raw offsets.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: padding split to expose xC float array
raw_diff: itCommonItems.h pad_xC[0x28] to float xC[10]
raw_diff: itsscope.c uses attrs->xC[charge_level]

accepted_candidate_records.title: Update owning headers when decompiled bodies recover signatures
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2291 paired the new C implementations of it_80291F14 and it_80291FA8 with itsscope.h prototype updates, and also recovered it_80291D38 as int(Item_GObj*, int).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itsscope.h prototype changes
raw_diff: itsscope.c implementations

source_update_candidates.title: Super Scope item code and header locations
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2291 provides evidence that Super Scope item implementation work touched src/melee/it/items/itsscope.c and src/melee/it/items/itsscope.h, while the shared item attributes struct itSScopeAttributes is in src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h.
evidence_refs: changed_files: three changed files listed
raw_diff: file paths and edits
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a path/scope fact candidate, not an accepted global lesson.

source_update_candidates.title: itSScopeAttributes offset 0xC recovered as float xC[10]
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2291 changed itSScopeAttributes at offset +0xC from pad_xC[0x28] to float xC[10], with +0x34 remaining Vec3 x34. The PR body states this split exposed the xC float array.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itCommonItems.h struct change
human_pr_text: padding split to expose xC float array
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential data-sheet/struct-layout update for curator review; semantics beyond field type and offset should remain cautious.

source_update_candidates.title: PAD_STACK must keep verifier evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2291 used PAD_STACK(16) and PAD_STACK(8) in newly matched functions and included PR/body bot evidence of 100% matches. This supports the existing standard that stack-padding tactics need narrow verifier evidence, but does not require a new standard.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK in it_80291F14 and it_80291FA8
human_pr_text: all functions at 100% match via objdiff
text_corpus: bot report lists both functions at 100.00%
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate note for standards curation; current loaded standards already cover matching tactics needing evidence.

rejection_notes.text: Do not promote a semantic name for itSScopeAttributes.xC or Item.xD4C from this PR alone; the slice shows usage as cost/charge-related storage but does not prove canonical field meaning.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: field remains address-style xC and item field remains xD4C
